summary: Cathy O'Neil announces the upcoming release of 'Occupy Finance,' a book written collaboratively by the Alt Banking Occupy group, planned for distribution on the second anniversary of Occupy Wall Street. The post serves as both an announcement and a grassroots fundraising appeal to raise $350 to print 1,000 copies. The book aims to explain financial system dysfunction in plain English for the 99% who sense something is wrong but lack the vocabulary to articulate it. O'Neil humorously acknowledges Occupy's lack of financial sophistication while outlining the book's structure covering financialization's impact, how the crisis happened, and actionable solutions.
key_points: The book is modeled after Strike Debt's 'Debt Resistor's Operations Manual' and written by committee through the Alt Banking Occupy group
The mission is to translate complex financial concepts into accessible language and give ordinary people 'ammunition at the water cooler'
The book covers three main areas: real-life impacts of financialization, historical context for how we got here, and concrete actions people can take
O'Neil is personally fronting the printing costs and running what she calls a 'crappy Kickstarter' through personal checks
The target audience is 'Occupy-friendly or at least Occupy-inquisitive' members of the 99%
money_quotes: This is Occupy after all, we suck at money. Consider this a crappy version of Kickstarter.
The mission of the book, like the mission of the Alt Banking group, is to explain the financial system and its dysfunction in plain English.
We want people who know there's something wrong, but don't have the background to articulate what it is, to have a reference to help them define their issues.
We want to give them ammunition at the water cooler.
Heads They Win, Tails We Lose: Real Life Impact of Financialization on the 99%
The bailout: it didn't work, it's still going on, and it's making things worse.
key_insight: Financial literacy is a form of political empowerment, and demystifying Wall Street's dysfunction for ordinary people is itself an act of resistance.
